authority_model.name: sqlite_sole_canonical_authority
authority_model.summary: The embedded SQLite database file is the sole canonical authoritative store. Canonical session entities are persisted in SQLite tables and committed only through explicit SQLite transactions. No external JSON file or derived export may serve as authority on open, reopen, or recovery.

derive_queue_index_v1.algorithm: For each review_cases row, find max sequence review_ledger_events row for matching case_id from payload_json; head_event_id is null when no events exist for case; build JSON array [{case_id, head_event_id|null}] sorted by case_id ascending
derive_queue_index_v1.empty_case_rule: cases with zero ledger events produce head_event_id null, not omitted rows
derive_queue_index_v1.content_hash: SHA-256 lowercase hex of value_encoding UTF-8 bytes
derive_queue_index_v1.validation: stored derived_cache.content_hash must equal recomputed hash or row is rebuilt before open completes

optional_json_export.import_protocol: none_in_spike; explicit separate protocol required before any restore use

prohibited_authorities: session.export.json or any external JSON snapshot as canonical source
adapter-returned object without persisted reopen for evidence claims
filesystem copy of session.db without Online Backup API or equivalent consistent snapshot
WAL/SHM triple-copy as ad hoc duplication protocol
derived_cache rows as canonical history substitute

authoritative_state_units.note: concurrency metadata; not domain canonical history

canonical_schema_ddl.session_meta: CREATE TABLE session_meta (key TEXT PRIMARY KEY, value TEXT NOT NULL);
canonical_schema_ddl.source_revisions: CREATE TABLE source_revisions (revision_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, payload_json TEXT NOT NULL);
canonical_schema_ddl.review_cases: CREATE TABLE review_cases (case_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, payload_json TEXT NOT NULL);
canonical_schema_ddl.review_ledger_events: CREATE TABLE review_ledger_events (event_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, sequence INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE, payload_json TEXT NOT NULL);
canonical_schema_ddl.review_case_raised_events: CREATE TABLE review_case_raised_events (event_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, sequence INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE, payload_json TEXT NOT NULL);
canonical_schema_ddl.analysis_results: CREATE TABLE analysis_results (analysis_result_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, payload_json TEXT NOT NULL);
canonical_schema_ddl.active_analysis_selection: CREATE TABLE active_analysis_selection (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY CHECK (id = 1), selection_json TEXT);
canonical_schema_ddl.knowledge_snapshot_references: CREATE TABLE knowledge_snapshot_references (knowledge_snapshot_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, payload_json TEXT NOT NULL);
canonical_schema_ddl.lineage_conflicts: CREATE TABLE lineage_conflicts (conflict_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, payload_json TEXT NOT NULL);
canonical_schema_ddl.artifacts: CREATE TABLE artifacts (artifact_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, retention_class TEXT NOT NULL, payload_json TEXT NOT NULL);
canonical_schema_ddl.retention_references: CREATE TABLE retention_references (reference_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, payload_json TEXT NOT NULL);
canonical_schema_ddl.derived_cache: CREATE TABLE derived_cache (key TEXT PRIMARY KEY, value TEXT NOT NULL, schema_version INTEGER NOT NULL, content_hash TEXT NOT NULL);
canonical_schema_ddl.writer_ownership: CREATE TABLE writer_ownership (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY CHECK (id = 1), token TEXT, owner_epoch INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, lease_duration_ms INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 30000, lease_expires_at_unix_ms INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, process_instance_id TEXT NOT NULL, holder_pid INTEGER);
foreign_keys: SQLite PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON enabled per connection
Spike schema uses payload_json columns without inter-table FK constraints
Referential integrity for nested entity IDs is validated in application load/assemble only
canonical_schema_ddl.identity_update_on_duplication: UPDATE session_meta SET value = ?1 WHERE key = 'session_id'; UPDATE session_meta SET value = ?2 WHERE key = 'duplicated_from';

transaction_boundary.create_scope: schema initialization and initial canonical rows in one transaction
transaction_boundary.duplication_identity_mutation_scope: separate transaction on destination temp database after backup integrity_check and before semantic validation and publish
transaction_boundary.migration_scope: future; single transaction with pre-migration backup required when implemented

layers.meaning: SQLite COMMIT returned success; authoritative rows visible to a new connection read
layers.note: COMMIT alone is not the API acknowledgement point

layers.meaning: Rust API Ok after commit and post-commit authoritative read verification
Caller received Ok; distinct from crash between commit and return

authoritative_command_success_requires: authoritative mutations applied in SQLite transaction
post-commit authoritative read matches expected mutation (2B test)

read_path.normalized_state_assembly: SELECT from canonical tables; assemble NormalizedSemanticState in memory
read_path.integrity_check_on_writable_open: PRAGMA integrity_check before writer ownership acquisition
read_path.integrity_check_on_read_only_open: PRAGMA integrity_check; fail closed if canonical tables unreadable

requires: handle token and owner_epoch match writer_ownership row
semantic precondition validation against loaded canonical state
sequence: revalidate writer token and owner_epoch inside transaction immediately before mutation
renew writer lease columns in same transaction
post-commit authoritative read verify on same or new read connection
return Ok only after verify succeeds

verify_fail_after_commit.authority_state: mutation committed in SQLite; caller must not treat as successful ack
verify_fail_after_commit.evidence_owner: 2C for crash between COMMIT and verify; 2B surfaces error only

write_path.failure_blocks_further_writes_until: validated writable state restored or session closed

does_not_trust: orphaned WAL copy without SQLite recovery

recovery_path.authoritative_source: SQLite engine recovery of session.db (+ WAL replay)

recovery_classification_surface.2b_scope: Surface recovery outcomes via existing AdapterError.code and OptionalOperationOutcome; no adapter trait change in 2B

error_code_mapping.canonical-corruption: Unrecoverable or ReadOnlySalvage depending on salvage path

duplication_semantics.operation_order: backup_snapshot -> integrity_check -> identity_transaction -> independent_semantic_validation -> wal_finalize_and_close -> atomic_publish -> post_publish_verification

writer_ownership_semantics.process_identity: process_instance_id UUID generated once per adapter process instance; optional pid diagnostic only
writer_ownership_semantics.acquisition: transactional claim when token IS NULL or lease expired
writer_ownership_semantics.renewal: on each authoritative command and explicit heartbeat interval
writer_ownership_semantics.stale_detection: lease_expires_at_unix_ms elapsed using system UTC milliseconds at open/acquire; never PID absence alone
writer_ownership_semantics.split_brain_prevention: second writer fails while valid lease active; epoch bump on takeover

sqlite_engine_configuration.features_note: 2B must add rusqlite backup feature to Cargo.toml persistence-spike dependency

settings.name: journal_mode
settings.semantic_purpose: Allow readers during writer; standard spike concurrency model
settings.cross_platform_note: WAL supported on Windows and macOS; verify behavior in 2D

settings.name: synchronous
settings.semantic_purpose: Engine sync setting; does not define API acknowledgement point
settings.cross_platform_note: FULL semantics may differ slightly by platform filesystem; 2D records differences

settings.name: foreign_keys
settings.semantic_purpose: Enforce FK constraints when present; spike tables use payload_json without inter-table FK DDL
does_not_prove: broken-reference detection for all corruption classes

settings.name: busy_timeout
settings.semantic_purpose: Bounded wait on SQLITE_BUSY during contention

settings.name: locking_strategy
settings.semantic_purpose: Engine file lock composes with application writer lease
settings.cross_platform_note: Windows sharing flags may differ; 2D required

settings.name: checkpoint_policy
settings.value: passive_on_writable_close; optional TRUNCATE before backup when policy requires
settings.semantic_purpose: Control WAL size; support consistent backup snapshot

settings.name: connection_lifecycle
settings.value: one writer Connection held for writable handle lifetime
settings.semantic_purpose: Stable transaction boundary and backup source handle

settings.name: transaction_mode
settings.value: DEFERRED explicit BEGIN/COMMIT per authoritative command
settings.semantic_purpose: Transaction groups authoritative mutation; API acknowledgement occurs only after post-commit verify

unsupported_claims.reason: Not claimed by 2A or 2B; pragma alone insufficient
macOS-only spike evidence insufficient per MD-015

implementation_invariants.statement: SQLite is the sole canonical authority.
implementation_invariants.2b_enforcement: Remove canonical_state.state_json blob authority; all canonical reads from SQLite tables

implementation_invariants.statement: No JSON-derived artifact can restore or override authority.
implementation_invariants.2b_enforcement: Disable session.export.json in 2B; open path never reads external JSON

implementation_invariants.statement: Every acknowledged authoritative command corresponds to a committed SQLite transaction.
implementation_invariants.2b_enforcement: Return Ok only after COMMIT and post-commit read verify succeed

implementation_invariants.statement: Transaction commit is not automatically classified as filesystem or hardware durability.
implementation_invariants.2b_enforcement: Document ack cap at LogicalStateTransition in adapter errors/results

implementation_invariants.statement: Duplication uses a consistent SQLite snapshot protocol.
implementation_invariants.2b_enforcement: Replace fs::copy with rusqlite backup::Backup API

implementation_invariants.statement: Source and destination identities are distinct and validated.
implementation_invariants.2b_enforcement: Identity mutation in destination tx; reopen validation test

implementation_invariants.statement: A crashed writer cannot permanently block future writers.
implementation_invariants.2b_enforcement: Lease expiry and explicit stale_takeover path

implementation_invariants.statement: Stale takeover cannot create two simultaneous authoritative writers.
implementation_invariants.2b_enforcement: Epoch-guarded transactional ownership replace; second writer fails
Map open/recovery failures to AdapterError.code per recovery_classification_surface; scenario_runner maps to RecoveryClassification in 2C

implementation_invariants.statement: Unknown future schema versions fail closed.
implementation_invariants.2b_enforcement: Reject writable open when format_version > CURRENT_FORMAT_VERSION

implementation_invariants.statement: Fault points identify whether authority changed before failure.
implementation_invariants.2b_enforcement: Extend FaultPoint enum with before/after authority metadata

implementation_invariants.statement: Scenario evidence cannot exceed the physical operation and observation executed.

alternatives_analysis.name: SQLite sole authority; JSON derived or removed
alternatives_analysis.crash_recovery: delegated to SQLite WAL + application ownership protocol

alternatives_analysis.name: JSON sole authority; SQLite derived index
alternatives_analysis.rejection_rationale: Violates MD-014 canonical state rules; duplicates append candidate without benefit

alternatives_analysis.name: Hybrid dual-artifact authority
alternatives_analysis.md_014_md_015_fit: violates single authority and ack rules
alternatives_analysis.rejection_rationale: No unambiguous commit authority; no product need; current blob+rewrite path already shows recovery hazard

material_distinctness.sqlite_mechanism: engine-managed relational/transactional canonical authority with WAL and Online Backup API
material_distinctness.distinctness_basis: authority model and recovery semantics not file format alone
material_distinctness.blocker_if_2b_fails: If 2B retains monolithic JSON blob as effective authority without relational decomposition, record blocker for mechanism distinctness reassessment

readiness_and_selection.note: 2A design contract does not change readiness; evidence execution required in 2C/2D
